author: @leoantonio
text: Why do the British add a syllable to “aluminum” that doesn’t exist?

replies.author: @simonhopkins3867
replies.text:
Is it spelled aluminum or aluminium?
The most comforting part of this story is that both Aluminum and Aluminium are accepted and correct. Aluminum is preferred in North America, whereas Aluminium is the spelling preferred in the United Kingdom and most other English-speaking nations.
Copy and pasted.

text: Not sure if I can find a fire extinguisher like that, but maybe a large diameter PVC pipe would work?  Does the roller need the weight of the fire extinguisher or is the round shape enough?  Maybe fill the the pipe with concrete if the weight is needed?

author: @netikras
text:
It feels like you're using that fire extinguisher for anything but extinguishing fire :) 
- an aluminium sheet curving tool
- a sand container for sandblasting
- I _think_ you used its contents for some experiments... I may be wrong on that one

Did you ever have to use it for the purpose God created it for? :) The fire extinguisher, I mean

author: @kennethjessop6073
text: I managed to roll a flue pipe from an 8x4 sheet of 1.5mm steel by rolling a large carpet up, then feeding the sheet into the roll and keep rolling. Need three people to turn it round but we ended up with a  14” dia. x 8foot pipe.
